children.heading: Imposition and Continuation of Suspensions.—
children.label: a Imposition and Continuation of Suspensions.—
A
children.text: if an administrative law judge appointed under , United States Code, issues a preliminary decision in a proceeding under section 6712(d)(1) that the government is not likely to prevail in showing compliance with section 6711(a) and (b);section 3105 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: if the administrative law judge decides at the end of the proceeding that the government has not complied with section 6711(a) or (b), unless the government makes a compliance agreement under section 6714 by the 30th day after the decision; or

children.heading: .—Effectiveness
children.label: 2 .—Effectiveness
children.text: A suspension already ordered under paragraph (1)(A) continues in effect if the administrative law judge makes a decision under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: .—Lifting of Suspensions and Terminations
children.label: b .—Lifting of Suspensions and Terminations
children.text: If a holding of discrimination is reversed by an appellate court, a suspension or termination of payments in a proceeding based on the holding shall be discontinued.

children.heading: .—Resumption of Payments Upon Attaining Compliance
children.label: c .—Resumption of Payments Upon Attaining Compliance
A
children.text: the approval by the Secretary of a compliance agreement under section 6714(a)(1); or

children.label: B
children.text: a compliance agreement entered into by the Secretary under section 6714(a)(2);

if the government complies completely with an order of a United States court, a State court, or administrative law judge that covers all matters raised in a notice of noncompliance submitted by the Secretary under section 6712(a);

if a United States court, a State court, or an administrative law judge decides (including a judge in a proceeding under section 6712(d)(1)), that the government has complied with sections 6711(a) and (b); or1So in original. Probably should be “section”.1
if a suspension is discontinued under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Payment of Damages as Compliance
children.label: d .—Payment of Damages as Compliance
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(2), compliance by a government may consist of the payment of restitution to a person injured because the government did not comply with section 6711(a) or (b).

children.heading: .—Resumption of Payments Upon Reversal by Court
children.label: e .—Resumption of Payments Upon Reversal by Court
children.text: The Secretary may resume payment to a unit of general local government of payments terminated under section 6712(d)(2)(A) only if the decision resulting in the termination is reversed by an appellate court.
